title: Watch: Bill Belichick wins a challenge during Savannah Bananas coaching debut
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Sports News The Bananas made a trip to UNC and got the former Patriots coach to throw a yellow flag.
Bill Belichick and the Tar Heels closed out the 2025 season with a 4-8 record.

AP Photo/Karl DeBlaker The Savannah Bananas made a trip to Chapel Hill for an exhibition game on Saturday night, and they didn’t hesitate to make a certain former Patriots coach turned Tar Heel a part of the action.

title: Review and setlist: Lily Allen asks ‘Who the f— is Madeline?’ at the Orpheum

title: Winthrop man charged with Social Security fraud, concealment
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The man allegedly concealed a person’s death from the government so he could fraudulently receive their retirement benefits.
By Darin Zullo April 12, 2026 | 11:00 AM 1 minute to read A Winthrop man was charged Friday for allegedly committing Social Security fraud for over four years, according to prosecutors.
Kenneth Riel, 51, was indicted on one count of receiving stolen government money or property and one count of Social Security fraud-concealment, the U.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 1-0 win over D.C. United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution New England walked a tightrope in the second half, but Matt Turner ensured there was no letdown.
Alhassan Yusuf celebrates after scoring for the Revolution in the 1-0 win over D.
C.

title: Former VA employee charged with fraudulently obtaining COVID-19 benefits
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The Brockton man allegedly applied for loans using fraudulent tax documents and making false claims about his revenues.

By Darin Zullo April 12, 2026 | 10:02 AM 1 minute to read A former Veterans Affairs employee was charged in federal court Thursday for allegedly fraudulently obtaining COVID-19 benefits, prosecutors said.
Jesus Abreu, 38, of Brockton, was indicted on three counts of wire fraud, the U.

title: Chelsea man sentenced for stealing mail, fraudulently depositing checks
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The man was one of nine members of the “Mission Hill Gang” involved in the scheme and charged in 2024, prosecutors said.

By Darin Zullo April 12, 2026 | 9:22 AM 1 minute to read A Chelsea man was sentenced in federal court Friday for his role in a scheme to fraudulently deposit checks he stole from the mail, according to prosecutors.
Josman Romero-Delgado, 24, pleaded guilty in November 2025 to charges of conspiracy to commit bank fraud, bank fraud, and conspiracy to steal and possess stolen mail, the U.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Colman Domingo hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Entertainment Colman Domingo hosted the April 11 episode of "Saturday Night Live," with Anitta as musical guest.
SNL recap: Host Colman Domingo appears before the April 11, 2026 episode of "Saturday Night Live.

title: Ranger Suarez’s scoreless start headlines Red Sox’ well-rounded 7-1 win vs. Cardinals
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox Boston scored five runs in the ninth inning to even the weekend series in St.
Louis.
Third time’s the charm for Ranger Suarez, who notched his first quality start in a Red Sox uniform Saturday.

title: Fleet’s sold-out homecoming at TD Garden a resounding victory for women’s sports in Boston
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: PWHL "I think they saw that they can play in a professional hockey league and they can sell out buildings that the men play in.
